passage.title: Group Project Update
passage.text: A project helper group hoped first graders could share jobs with less confusion. At first, the helpers posted long role lists that placed several jobs in one area, but the role boxes were hard to compare quickly. A practice group said "There are many names here, and the roles feel mixed together." After hearing that, the team moved the steps into a clear first-second-third order and used the same color family for one team at a time. They also hung a reminder board beside the supply shelf so children received another clue when they forgot the order of the jobs. On the next test day, the younger class shared the jobs with fewer mix-ups after the redesign. The team noticed that students needed the order of team steps to stand out first. Because of that result, helpers chose to redesign more teamwork boards with the clear format.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
Redesigning a group project board for first graders
Painting a room with brighter colors
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: Students could not tell what to do next
The role boxes were hard to compare quickly
The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the helpers set in the middle of the room?
choices: A quick-check sign
Larger arrows
Short signs
Color blocks
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does decorative mean in the seventh sentence?
choices: A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
Made mainly to look pretty
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
Students could match the table cards with the main board more easily
It made the room much larger
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Ask students to memorize everything
Redesign more teamwork boards with the new format
Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
